methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $44M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Glyphic Biotechnologies is a Series A biotech company founded by Joshua Yang and Daniel Estandian that has raised $44M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 8 private and 3 public companies in the Biotech & Health sector, including peers such as Eikon Therapeutics, Xaira Therapeutics, Neko Health, Asimov, and New Limit across seed through Series B stages.

Two triangulation methods are applicable to this analysis. The stage-progression method applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $88M based on the $44M total raised as a proxy for current valuation. The peer-median method anchors to a cohort of 7 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, which exhibit a median valuation of $810M with a range of $100M to $3.3B. Public company benchmarking via price-to-sales multiples (median 6.1x across 3 Biotech tickers) cannot be applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue figures for the target.

The triangulated valuation range is $75M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $449M. The wide dispersion reflects substantial stage and risk heterogeneity within the peer set. Key uncertainties include the target's current clinical and commercial progress, which would clarify positioning relative to the lower-valued seed-stage peers versus late-stage comparables commanding $1B+ valuations.
